---

title: "Join and Load to Warehouse"
author: "Fabricio Lennart"
date: "2026-04-03"
format: html
jupyter: python3
execute:
    echo: true
    warning: false
    message: false
---

## Overview

This notebook joins products and carts data, then loads the result:

* Read products and carts from the Lakehouse
* Join both tables on product_id
* Save the joined table for Warehouse ingestion

---

## Step 1: Read Data from Lakehouse

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df_products = pd.read_csv('../data/products.csv')
df_carts = pd.read_csv('../data/carts.csv')

print(f"Products: {len(df_products)} rows")
print(f"Carts: {len(df_carts)} rows")
```

---

## Step 2: Join Tables

```{python}
df_joined = df_carts.merge(
    df_products,
    left_on="product_id",
    right_on="id",
    how="left"
)

print(f"Joined: {len(df_joined)} rows")
df_joined.head()
```

---

## Step 3: Save to Lakehouse

```{python}
df_joined.to_csv('../data/fact_cart_products.csv', index=False)

print("Table fact_cart_products saved to Lakehouse")
```

---

## Notes

* The joined table combines cart transactions with product details
* This table is loaded to the Warehouse via Copy Data in the pipeline
* The pipeline schedule automates the full extraction and load process
